commit.message: Bump version: 0.12.3 → 0.12.4

fix(_BatchWriter): write all labels for edges (#480)

* fix(_BatchWriter): manage label orders for nodes or edges

Before, only nodes were having their labels exported and honoring labels_order.
Now, PostgreSQL can use (node|edge)_labels_order option, instead of a single labels_order.
Specific options takes precedence over labels_order.

The Neo4j driver always set edge_labels_order: Leaves.
The RDF & OWL drivers always set everything to Leaves.

Refs: PR #480

* phrasing / grammar

* wording

---------

Co-authored-by: Sebastian Lobentanzer <sebastian.lobentanzer@gmail.com>

fix(_BatchWriter): write all labels for edges (#480)

* fix(_BatchWriter): manage label orders for nodes or edges

Before, only nodes were having their labels exported and honoring labels_order.
Now, PostgreSQL can use (node|edge)_labels_order option, instead of a single labels_order.
Specific options takes precedence over labels_order.

The Neo4j driver always set edge_labels_order: Leaves.
The RDF & OWL drivers always set everything to Leaves.

Refs: PR #480

* phrasing / grammar

* wording

commit.message: Bump version: 0.12.0 → 0.12.1

fix(core/write_import_call): display an error message when no element is added (#464)

Updated CI workflow to use MacOS latest, since 13 is no longer supported.

* fix(core/write_import_call): display an error message when no element is added

And tries to continue with calling the initialized writer.

* convert error to warning (as we continue execution)

* format

* update macos to `latest` (13 is retired)

---------

Co-authored-by: Sebastian Lobentanzer <sebastian.lobentanzer@gmail.com>

commit.message: remove Paraguay flag from autogenerated API documentation
remove accidentally added submodule
Bump version: 0.11.0 → 0.12.0

Migrate neo4j_utils functionality into BioCypher and make Neo4j optional (#470)

* Migrate neo4j_utils functionality into BioCypher and make Neo4j optional

Migrate the unmaintained neo4j_utils package functionality into BioCypher
as an internal module, modernize for Neo4j 5.x compatibility, and make
the Neo4j driver an optional dependency.

Major changes:
- Create internal Neo4jDriver wrapper module (biocypher/output/connect/_neo4j_driver_wrapper.py)
- Migrate all neo4j_utils.Driver functionality with improved error handling
- Update BioCypher Neo4j driver to use internal module
- Make Neo4j driver imports conditional (only when Neo4j DBMS is used)
- Remove neo4j-utils dependency, add neo4j>=5.0 as optional dependency
- Add Neo4j 5.x compatibility (constraint syntax, database parameters)
- Add APOC availability checks with graceful degradation
- Fix test fixtures to prevent driver reuse issues
- Add toml dependency (was missing)
- Improve error messages and driver state checks

Breaking changes: None - drop-in replacement with same API

Users can now install with:
- pip install biocypher (no Neo4j driver required)
- pip install biocypher[neo4j] (includes Neo4j driver for online mode)

* add docs for neo4j driver migration

* formatting

* Fix Neo4j 5.x database creation and query parameters

- Fix db_status() and _manage_db() to use system database for management queries
- Fix session.run() to pass parameters via 'parameters' argument instead of unpacking kwargs
- Ensures database creation works correctly in Neo4j 5.x before connecting

* formatting

* Fix has_apoc to always return boolean

- Add early checks in apoc_version for offline/closed driver
- Catch RuntimeError and other exceptions in apoc_version
- Ensure has_apoc always returns bool even if apoc_version raises
- Prevents crashes when checking APOC availability

* Fix ruff E402 error: ignore module import order

- Add ruff noqa comment for E402 rule
- Imports after bcolors class definition are intentional

* noqa for import

* Improve database creation error handling and GitHub Actions setup

- Add comprehensive error handling and logging to ensure_db()
- Ensure database exists before wipe_db() operations
- Add Neo4j readiness check in GitHub Actions workflow
- Raise exceptions if database creation/start fails to prevent silent failures

* formatting

* Fix db_status to handle DatabaseNotFound gracefully in Neo4j 5.x

- Pass raise_errors=False to query() in db_status() to prevent exceptions
- Handle None response as database not existing (expected behavior)
- Fixes issue where Neo4j 5.x routing protocol fails when checking
  if a database exists before creating it

* format

* revert fallback to pre-migration to see if that fixes the CI error

* Add automatic Community Edition detection and fallback

- Detect Neo4j Community Edition using dbms.components()
- Convert neo4j:// to bolt:// for Community Edition to avoid routing issues
- Automatically disable multi_db mode for Community Edition
- Fall back to default database 'neo4j' if custom database requested
- Skip database creation/start operations for Community Edition
- Makes BioCypher work seamlessly with both CE and EE without manual config

* format

* Fix Community Edition detection to use bolt:// for detection query

- Use temporary bolt:// driver for edition detection to avoid routing issues
- Add safeguard in ensure_db() to skip database checks for bolt:// connections
- Ensures detection completes before ensure_db() runs

* format

* Make ensure_db() more defensive for Community Edition detection

- Add error handling for URI conversion during detection
- Skip database existence check if neo4j:// protocol with non-default database
- Assumes Community Edition when detection fails or URI conversion fails

* format

* Fix wipe_db() to handle Community Edition with non-default database

- Check for Community Edition before wiping database
- Use default database 'neo4j' if trying to wipe non-default database in CE
- Prevents DatabaseNotFound errors when wiping 'test' database in Community Edition

* format

* Fix wipe_db() to permanently use 'neo4j' in Community Edition

- Don't restore database back to 'test' after wiping in Community Edition
- Keep database as 'neo4j' permanently when Community Edition is detected
- Prevents subsequent queries from failing with DatabaseNotFound

* format

* fix indentation

* Fix query() to always set database parameter when explicitly configured

- Always set database parameter if multi_db is False but explicit database is set
- Ensures Community Edition uses correct database even when multi_db is disabled
- Fixes issue where _update_meta_graph() was using wrong database after wipe_db()

* format

* Add --neo4j_enterprise flag to force Enterprise Edition mode

- Add CLI flag to skip Community Edition detection for local Enterprise testing
- Default to Community Edition when detection fails (safer for CI)
- Pass flag through fixtures to driver initialization

* Revert default to Community Edition - restore original detection behavior

- Don't apply Community Edition workarounds when detection fails
- Allows Enterprise Edition to work normally in CI
- --neo4j_enterprise flag still works for local Enterprise testing

* Restore Community Edition assumption on detection failure

- Align with commit 7d725c6 behavior
- When detection fails, assume Community Edition (safer for CI)
- Only skip detection if --neo4j_enterprise flag is set

* Restore query() and wipe_db() to match commit 7d725c6

- Remove extra database parameter logic from query()
- Restore original wipe_db() Community Edition detection
- Keep force_enterprise flag support for local testing

* format

* Handle explicit db parameter in Community Edition

- When multi_db is False, override non-default db parameter to use current_db
- Fixes test_increment_version failing with DatabaseNotFound error
- Applies fix before setting session database parameter

* Respect force_enterprise flag in wipe_db() and ensure_db()

- Skip Community Edition checks in wipe_db() when force_enterprise is True
- Skip Community Edition checks in ensure_db() when force_enterprise is True
- Prevents switching to 'neo4j' database when Enterprise Edition is forced
- Fixes issue where force_enterprise flag was ignored in wipe_db() and ensure_db()

* Fix test_increment_version to not pass explicit db parameter

- Remove db='test' parameter from query() calls in test_increment_version
- Test now uses default database (set to 'neo4j' in Community Edition)
- Aligns with other tests that don't pass explicit db parameter
- Removes unnecessary Community Edition db override logic from query()

Bump version: 0.10.1 → 0.11.0

feat: dedicated graph class and simplified agent-usable API (#459)

* add graph class and agentic API (vibe code draft)

* unused import

* add todos

* todo

* todo

* update test (python version?) that fails on github CI

* remove failing test for now

* skip scirpy tests if not installed

* fix json serialisation

* reintroduce removed test

* linting, formatting

* docs formatting

* quick fix for remote install issue

* switch to uv for dependency management

* update actions to uv (untested)

* refactor: rename BioCypherAgent to BioCypherWorkflow for better semantic clarity

This commit renames the agent-focused API to a more flexible workflow-oriented API
that better reflects its purpose and future extensibility.

## Key Changes

### API Renaming
- BioCypherAgent → BioCypherWorkflow
- create_knowledge_graph() → create_workflow()
- Default graph name: 'knowledge_graph' → 'workflow_graph'

### File Structure
- biocypher/_agent.py → biocypher/_workflow.py
- test/test_agent.py → test/test_workflow.py

### Updated Documentation
- All docstrings updated to reflect workflow terminology
- Examples updated to use new API names
- Documentation files updated (agent_api_guide.md, graph_class_guide.md, installation.md)

### Code Changes
- Updated all import statements in __init__.py
- Renamed all class references throughout codebase
- Updated all example files to use new API
- Updated all test files and test class names
- Updated all docstring examples and references

## Rationale

The new naming better reflects the API's purpose:
- 'Workflow' is more flexible than 'Agent' - supports both agentic and deterministic use cases
- 'create_workflow()' clearly indicates the function's purpose
- 'BioCypherWorkflow' is distinct from the main BioCypher class
- Future-proof naming that doesn't lock into agent-specific terminology

## Verification
- All imports work correctly with new names
- Examples run successfully with new API
- Tests pass with updated naming
- No breaking changes to core functionality

This change addresses feedback about the misleading 'Agent' terminology while
maintaining all existing functionality and improving semantic clarity.

* cleanup

* feat: enhance BioCypherWorkflow with progressive validation and comprehensive documentation

Major enhancements to the new Workflow API:

## Core Improvements
- Add progressive validation modes (none/warn/strict) for flexible use cases
- Implement optional deduplication with configurable behavior
- Add schema validation with property type checking
- Enhance error handling and logging for validation failures

## API Enhancements
- Add validation_mode and deduplication parameters to create_workflow()
- Implement _validate_property_type() for schema validation
- Add runtime validation mode switching capability
- Improve add_node() and add_edge() with validation integration

## Documentation Overhaul
- Create comprehensive architecture migration guide (architecture-migration.md)
- Add detailed API reference documentation for BioCypherWorkflow and Graph classes
- Merge and enhance Agent API Guide with validation examples and best practices
- Add prominent limitations section for legacy BioCypher users
- Update navigation structure in mkdocs.yml and mkdocs.nav.yml

## Testing
- Add comprehensive validation mode test suite (14 new tests)
- Test all validation modes, deduplication scenarios, and edge cases
- Add schema validation tests with property type checking
- Test runtime validation mode changes and parameter combinations

## Key Features
- Progressive validation: Start with 'none' for agents, add validation as needed
- Schema validation: Optional property type checking and required field validation
- Deduplication: Configurable duplicate detection with warning/error modes
- Compatibility: Maintains backward compatibility with existing API
- Documentation: Single comprehensive guide replacing overlapping tutorials

This update completes Phase 1 of the Workflow API implementation, providing
a robust foundation for both agentic and deterministic knowledge graph workflows.

* formatting

* remove redundant python setup

* change instructions / docs to `uv`

* format

* refactor: rename BioCypherAgent to BioCypherWorkflow for better semantic clarity

This commit renames the agent-focused API to a more flexible workflow-oriented API
that better reflects its purpose and future extensibility.

## Key Changes

### API Renaming
- BioCypherAgent → BioCypherWorkflow
- create_knowledge_graph() → create_workflow()
- Default graph name: 'knowledge_graph' → 'workflow_graph'

### File Structure
- biocypher/_agent.py → biocypher/_workflow.py
- test/test_agent.py → test/test_workflow.py

### Updated Documentation
- All docstrings updated to reflect workflow terminology
- Examples updated to use new API names
- Documentation files updated (agent_api_guide.md, graph_class_guide.md, installation.md)

### Code Changes
- Updated all import statements in __init__.py
- Renamed all class references throughout codebase
- Updated all example files to use new API
- Updated all test files and test class names
- Updated all docstring examples and references

## Rationale

The new naming better reflects the API's purpose:
- 'Workflow' is more flexible than 'Agent' - supports both agentic and deterministic use cases
- 'create_workflow()' clearly indicates the function's purpose
- 'BioCypherWorkflow' is distinct from the main BioCypher class
- Future-proof naming that doesn't lock into agent-specific terminology

## Verification
- All imports work correctly with new names
- Examples run successfully with new API
- Tests pass with updated naming
- No breaking changes to core functionality

This change addresses feedback about the misleading 'Agent' terminology while
maintaining all existing functionality and improving semantic clarity.

* cleanup

* feat: enhance BioCypherWorkflow with progressive validation and comprehensive documentation

Major enhancements to the new Workflow API:

## Core Improvements
- Add progressive validation modes (none/warn/strict) for flexible use cases
- Implement optional deduplication with configurable behavior
- Add schema validation with property type checking
- Enhance error handling and logging for validation failures

## API Enhancements
- Add validation_mode and deduplication parameters to create_workflow()
- Implement _validate_property_type() for schema validation
- Add runtime validation mode switching capability
- Improve add_node() and add_edge() with validation integration

## Documentation Overhaul
- Create comprehensive architecture migration guide (architecture-migration.md)
- Add detailed API reference documentation for BioCypherWorkflow and Graph classes
- Merge and enhance Agent API Guide with validation examples and best practices
- Add prominent limitations section for legacy BioCypher users
- Update navigation structure in mkdocs.yml and mkdocs.nav.yml

## Testing
- Add comprehensive validation mode test suite (14 new tests)
- Test all validation modes, deduplication scenarios, and edge cases
- Add schema validation tests with property type checking
- Test runtime validation mode changes and parameter combinations

## Key Features
- Progressive validation: Start with 'none' for agents, add validation as needed
- Schema validation: Optional property type checking and required field validation
- Deduplication: Configurable duplicate detection with warning/error modes
- Compatibility: Maintains backward compatibility with existing API
- Documentation: Single comprehensive guide replacing overlapping tutorials

This update completes Phase 1 of the Workflow API implementation, providing
a robust foundation for both agentic and deterministic knowledge graph workflows.

* formatting

* remove redundant python setup

* change instructions / docs to `uv`

* format

commit.message: Bump version: 0.9.7 → 0.10.0

Feat: Add AnnData KG in memory representation (#436)

This PR adds support for the creation of immune receptor binding information in the dedicated AIRR format for interoperability purposes in connecting to `scirpy`. An in-memory KG is built with specific adherence to the AIRR format and metadata for handoff to `scirpy` in `AirrCell` objects.

* Added a method to trim long filenames for the download functions

* Added a line in the end of the file and changed the allowed filename length

* Ruff formatting minor fixes

* Ruff fix

* Ran pre-commit hook locally

* Added Anndata in memory representation and initial test for it

* Refactore AIRR-to-AnnData KG in memory representation + add further tests

* Deleted unused packages

* Add poetry lock update

* Add a placeholder for anndata writer class + optional dependency

* Add missing import

* Install extra dependencies into CI env

* Fixed the issue with optional dependencies for CI pipeline

* Add ruff fixes

* Ran pre-commit

* generalise dependency message
(don't assume user uses Poetry)

* Create AIRR data from Biocypher for immunological data instead of AnnData

* Add test and writer for AIRR

* Update lock file

* Update test with a new attribute

* Modularise to_airr_cells function to make it more readable

* Fix formatting

* Fix the formatting

* Fix formatting with precommit

* Fix formatting with precommit

* Refactor AirrKG class to improve entity type handling and logging

* Update test cases for AIRR format

* Refactor AirrKG class to improve metadata handling for the case of several epitopes per IR pair and improve edge processing

* Add a count of IRs with more than one metadata enitity (epitope)

* Introduce an AIRR field for epitope validation status and add an option of indirect/direct connections in AIRR cells

* Fixed ruff errors

* update lock file

* more comprehensive test battery

* formatting

* fix ruff warnings

---------

commit.message: Bump version: 0.8.0 → 0.9.0
remove outdated caveat

feat(write): OWL (#412)

* feat(write): OWL

A writer able to output a self-contained populated OWL file:
- Based on RDFWriter.
- Embbed the input ontology within the output one.
- Using the input's namespaces.
- Allows two edge models:
    - using owl:ObjectProperty, but losing edge properties,
    - or using intermediate owl:Class, keeping edge properties.

Internal changes:
- Adds `get_rdf_graph` to Ontology.
- Rename _rdf:`subject_to_uri` to `to_uri`.
- Adds a compatible `as_uri` to deal with explicit namespaces.
- Adds `find_uri` for terms with unknown namespaces.

- Adds a documentation.
- Adds a test, following the RDF one.

* remove duplicated log statement
this was causing the linting error

BREAKING CHANGE: file_format replaces rdf_format

* explicit encoding in hopes of fixing the windows runner

* remove shebang

* feat: Add 'ttl' as a supported RDF format without conversion

- Add 'ttl' to the list of supported RDF formats
- Remove conversion from 'ttl' to 'turtle' as 'ttl' is directly supported by rdflib
- Add tests for both RDF and OWL writers with 'ttl' format
- Update docs to reflect addition

fixes Allow `ttl` as format spec #422

* remove unused variable

committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1741200576 +0100

feat(write): OWL (#412)

* feat(write): OWL

A writer able to output a self-contained populated OWL file:
- Based on RDFWriter.
- Embbed the input ontology within the output one.
- Using the input's namespaces.
- Allows two edge models:
    - using owl:ObjectProperty, but losing edge properties,
    - or using intermediate owl:Class, keeping edge properties.

Internal changes:
- Adds `get_rdf_graph` to Ontology.
- Rename _rdf:`subject_to_uri` to `to_uri`.
- Adds a compatible `as_uri` to deal with explicit namespaces.
- Adds `find_uri` for terms with unknown namespaces.

- Adds a documentation.
- Adds a test, following the RDF one.

readme_html:
BioCypher
License Python
Package Build status
Tests Docker
Development Contributions

❓ Description

Knowledge graphs (KGs) are an approach to knowledge representation that uses graph structure to facilitate exploration and analysis of complex data, often leveraging semantic information. They are popular in many research areas, including the life sciences, due to their versatile use, for instance in data storage, integration, reasoning, and more recently in artificial intelligence. The creation of KGs is a complex task; BioCypher helps you in creating and maintaining your own KG. For more overview, usage notes, and a tutorial, read the docs here.

📖 Documentation

Tutorial and developer docs at https://biocypher.org. For a quickstart into your own pipeline, you can refer to our project template, and for an overview of existing and planned adapters for resources and outputs, as well as other features, visit our GitHub Project Board.

⚙️ Installation / Usage

Install the package from PyPI using pip install biocypher. For Neo4j online mode support, install with pip install biocypher[neo4j]. More comprehensive installation and configuration instructions can be found here.

Exemplary usage of BioCypher to build a graph database is shown in our tutorial and the various pipelines we have created. You can find these on the Components Project Board.

🤝 Getting involved

We are very happy about contributions from the community, large and small! If you would like to contribute to BioCypher development, please refer to our contribution guidelines. :)

If you want to ask informal questions, talk about dev things, or just chat, please join our community at https://biocypher.zulipchat.com!

Imposter syndrome disclaimer: We want your help. No, really. There may be a little voice inside your head that is telling you that you're not ready, that you aren't skilled enough to contribute. We assure you that the little voice in your head is wrong. Most importantly, there are many valuable ways to contribute besides writing code.

This disclaimer was adapted from the Pooch project.

✍️ Citation

The BioCypher paper has been peer-reviewed in Nature Biotechnology. It is available as a self-archived version on Zenodo, online version here. Before, it was available as a preprint at https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.13543.

Acknowledgements

This project has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No 965193 for DECIDER and No 116030 for TransQST.
